title: Protocol 1: Mitigating Injection in Auth Architecture
whatToAvoid: Never expose raw auth operational logic to the client interface. The introduction of Injection vectors occurs when developers inherently trust un-sanitized boundaries. Failing to abstract Auth parameters fundamentally bypasses the entire zero-trust methodology required in modern SaaS.
whatToDo: Enforce a strict, typed boundary layer mediating all Auth requests. Validate schema at the gateway, sanitize inputs utilizing parameterized constraints, and ensure cryptographic non-repudiation against Injection patterns. Adopt defense-in-depth where Auth is completely isolated.
